mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase discount rates and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during recession onset.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit growth, producing positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative when both series trend upward.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which suppresses earnings expectations and yields negative forward equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in >55% of late-cycle observations, or the mechanism reverses direction during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth above 220.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or forward returns turn negative during periods of sustained payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production signals a services-led expansion where manufacturing drag is offset by broader economic growth, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative when GDP rises while industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts below 550 thousand units indicates a collapsing construction sector that reduces supply chain activity and corporate revenues, driving negative equity returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 550k coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in >65% of post-shock observations, or the signal loses predictive power outside recovery regimes.
